steps.title: Confirm scene safety
steps.description: Confirm scene is safe. Note any environmental hazards (smoke, chemical fumes, low oxygen environment). Don appropriate PPE.

steps.title: Assess airway patency
steps.description: Observe for signs of obstruction: stridor, wheeze, use of accessory muscles, tracheal tug, cyanosis. For choking: assess severity (mild vs. severe obstruction).

steps.title: Manage choking (if applicable)
steps.description: Mild obstruction: encourage coughing. Severe obstruction (unable to speak/cough/breathe): 5 back blows then 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich manoeuvre). Repeat cycle until object expelled or patient becomes unconscious.
steps.guideline_ref: Kenya BLS/ACLS Field Protocol — Foreign Body Airway Obstruction

steps.title: Provide breathing support
steps.description: Apply supplemental oxygen via appropriate device: nasal cannula (1-4L/min) for mild distress, non-rebreather mask (10-15L/min) for moderate-severe distress. Bag-valve mask if apnoeic.

steps.title: Position patient for comfort
steps.description: Position upright (sitting or semi-recumbent) unless contraindicated. Most respiratory distress patients breathe better sitting up. Allow tripod position if preferred.

steps.title: Record initial vitals
steps.description: Record: RR, SpO2 (note if on supplemental O2 and at what flow), BP, HR, consciousness (AVPU), temperature. Note work of breathing (accessory muscles, nasal flaring, inability to speak in full sentences).

steps.title: Obtain relevant medical history
steps.description: Ask about: asthma, COPD, recent illness, allergies, medications (especially inhalers, steroids, home oxygen), previous similar episodes, recent travel (PE risk).

steps.title: Reassess vitals (every 5 min)
steps.description: Repeat vitals every 5 minutes. Document SpO2 trend with and without supplemental O2. Alert receiving facility of deterioration.

steps.title: Determine disposition
steps.description: Record destination facility and transport mode. Ensure receiving facility notified of: SpO2 readings, work of breathing, medical history, and any treatments given.
